pr.title: fix: only suppress auto-append when caller explicitly provides a tool result
pr.body:
## Summary

Fixes #1536.

Calling `runner.append_messages()` with a plain user message inside the tool runner loop caused an infinite loop. Any call to `append_messages()` unconditionally set `_messages_modified = True`, which the runner interpreted as the caller manually handling the tool result. It would skip its own auto-append of the assistant message and tool result, so the next iteration sent a request with no tool result in history ‚Äî Claude saw the original question unanswered, re-issued the same tool call, and the loop never terminated.

## Root cause

`_messages_modified` was serving two distinct purposes with no way to tell them apart:

1. **Caller is adding extra context** (e.g. `append_messages({"role": "user", "content": "be concise"})`) ‚Äî runner should still auto-append the tool result
2. **Caller is manually providing the tool result** (e.g. `append_messages(assistant_msg, tool_result_msg)`) ‚Äî runner should skip auto-append

Both paths set the flag, so the runner always skipped auto-append regardless of intent.

## Fix

`_messages_modified` is now only set when at least one of the appended messages contains a `tool_result` content block ‚Äî the unambiguous signal that the caller is handling the tool result themselves. Appending messages without tool results leaves the flag untouched and the loop continues correctly.

## What this affects

- Sync runner (`BetaToolRunner`) ‚úÖ
- Async runner (`BetaAsyncToolRunner`) ‚úÖ ‚Äî both share the same `append_messages()` on the base class
- The working "Modifying tool results" pattern (passing both assistant message + tool result) continues to work unchanged
- `set_messages_params()` is unaffected ‚Äî it never touches the flag

## Tests

Added `test_append_messages_non_tool_result_does_not_suppress_auto_append` ‚Äî a pure unit test (no API calls required) that directly asserts the flag behaviour for both the plain-message case and the tool-result case.

The existing `test_custom_message_handling` (marked `xfail`) is a related but separate issue and remains untouched.
pr.author: Echolonius
